findings.title: Client callback used as financial truth
findings.message: Value is granted based on client-controlled state that can be forged.
findings.explanation: A browser redirect or client callback can be manipulated by the payer.
findings.remediation: Insert Verify Transaction → Validate Amount → Idempotency Guard before the fulfilment step.

findings.title: Missing webhook signature verification
findings.message: An attacker could forge a payment event and trigger business logic.
findings.explanation: Webhooks are unauthenticated until you validate the signature.
findings.remediation: Insert Verify Webhook Signature immediately after the webhook node.

findings.title: Immediate split contradicts payout-after-fulfilment
findings.message: Transaction split settles to the provider at payment time — before fulfilment is confirmed.
findings.explanation: Split disburses immediately; this workflow also gates payout on fulfilment.
findings.remediation: Replace Transaction Split with: Hold Funds → wait for Fulfilment → Initiate Transfer.

findings.title: Missing idempotency boundary
findings.message: A duplicate webhook or retry could create a duplicate financial effect.
findings.explanation: Providers may deliver a webhook more than once.
findings.remediation: Insert an Idempotency Guard keyed on the payment reference before the effect.
